Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Apr-25-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF CHROMADIVERSE, INC.'s Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

On June 8, 2023 Plaintiff Chromadiverse, Inc. and Defendant Dance Theatre of Harlem entered into a joint stipulation staying the matter pending resolution of related proceedings. In short, the terms of the agreement provided the proceedings in this court would be stayed for one year from the date of filing the joint stipulation and order to (1) determine whether Marbeth's estate needed to be probated to determine the rightful heir of the photograph collection and to find the appropriate forum for the proceedings, and (2) complete the probate proceedings, or initiate the action in the proper forum.

Nearly a year after the expiration of the stipulation, Chromadiverse seeks to lift the stay of proceedings because the parties entered into a new joint stipulation to lift the stay of proceedings on December 6, 2024. (Ex. B.) The parties' joint stipulation filing was rejected by the court on January 16, 2025 because a judge of this court determined a hearing following noticed motion was required. (Ex. C.) Chromadiverse contends that if the stay is not lifted, it will be prejudiced because two key witnesses in this matter are now in their mid-90s, the harms in this matter are ongoing, and further delay in this case will impede Chromadiverse's ability to obtain relief.

However, specially appearing defendant Dance Theatre filed a Response arguing that the matter should remain stayed because it filed a quasi in rem action on April 10, 2025 in the Southern District of New York ("SDNY") seeking a declaration that it is the exclusive owner of all rights to the Schnare archive which includes the Marbeth photograph collection. (Ex. 4) Dance Theatre contends the matter should be stayed pending the outcome of the SDNY action because the Marbeth photograph collection is within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Copyright Act, and the photographs at issue are located in New York. Chromadiverse argues the newly filed quasi in rem proceeding is not dispositive of all the issues in this case.

Generally, stay of the California proceedings is not a matter of right, but within the trial court's discretion. Here, however, the stay was effected not by a discretionary decision of this court but by an agreement of the parties that expired by its terms on June 9, 2024. Moreover, at least some of the claims at issue in this case will not be resolved by the SDNY action. Were the court deciding a stay motion in the first instance, where the burden would lie with the party seeking the stay, it would have to weigh hardships to the parties. Chromadiverse presents a factual case for hardship (the risk of unavailability of elderly witnesses), while Dance Theatre makes no such factual showing. The court therefore grants the motion and sets May 26, 2025 as Dance Theatre's deadline to respond to the complaint and/or summons. This order is without prejudice to any forum non conveniens or similar motion Dance Theatre may file.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
